rationale: The two papers are methodologically very close: the cited 2010 JMR paper introduces a paired-comparison compositional approach (PCPM) for complex products, while the citing 2011 paper proposes ASE for the same many-attribute preference-elicitation problem. The citing note says it positions ASE against prior adaptive/hybrid methods and evaluates predictive validity versus alternative elicitation approaches, so this citation is plausibly a substantive method source/comparison point rather than an incidental background mention.
